content.full_text:
Re
Fnrn PolrrrcAl Pnecrrcss CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
June 16,2010
Philip D. Kohn
Rutan & Tucker, LLP
P. O. Box 1950
Costa Mesa, CA92628
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-10-069
Dear Mr. Kohn:
Because you seek general guidance, we are providing informal assistance, rather than
advice. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an
opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83L14; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) In addition, ihis
letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("the
Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) I
FPPC Ops.71.)
Please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore
offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law
conflict of interest or Govemment Code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
May Commissioner Omar Ezzeldine participate in decisions of the Finance Commission
relating to the city's budget if he runs a sllmmer camp for children through the park and
recreational programs administered by the city's Community Services Departmlnt?
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
ret'erencesaretotheGovernmentCode,unlessotherwiseinclicated. Theregulationsofthefiirpotitical practices
Commissionarecontained inSections ltlll0throu-eh l8997of Title20f theCalifbrniaCodeof Regulations. All
regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Nothing in this letter should be constmed to
evaluate any conduct that may have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this
letter apply only to prospective actions.


File No. I-10-069
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
Based on the facts you provided, Commissioner Ezzeldine will not have a disqualifying
conflict of interest if the "public generally" exception applies as discussed below.
FACTS
You are the city attomey for the City of Irvine (the "city"). You are requesting informal
advice on behalf of Omar Ezzeldine, an appointed member of the Finance Commission of the
city. The City of Irvine has annual revenues in excess of $100,000,000 but less than
$400,000,000.
You wish to know whether Commissioner Ezzeldine's operation of the summer camp as
part of the city's park and recreation programs (through the city's Commgnity Services
Department) would create potential conflicts of interest for him in his duties as a member of the
Finance Commission.
Mt.Ezzeldine serves as an appointed member of the City of Irvine Finance Commission.
The Finance Commission, which was established by city council resolution in 1986, and is an
advisory body that makes recommendations to the City Council regarding: (l) the City's
proposed budget; (2) the appropriation of funds; (3) funding for activities and programs; (4)
potential revenue opportunities; (5) cost saving measures; (6) proposed support oior opposition
to state or federal legislation; and (7) other matters as requested by the city council.
Commissioner Ezzeldine is an educator by training. In that capacity, he has run a
summer camp in the city for children ages 3 to 11. Recently, the city'; Community Services
Department (CSD) inquired about the possibility of conduciing Mr.Ezzeldine's summer camp
through the city's parks and recreation programs.
Under a standard form agreement between the city and program providers, parents could
enroll their children for the summer camp through the city's ."gist*tion system und puy
registration fees directly to the city. The city, in turn, would thin write a check to Mr. Ezzeldine
for 80 percent portion ofthe fees collected, retaining20 percent for general revenue purposes.
By all estimates, the amount of fees Commissioner Ezzeldine would ieceive unde*u.t an
arrangement would exceed the $500, but would not exceed $5,000.
You also state that if "the City itself is deemed to be a financial interest of Commissioner
Ezzeldine, then we recognize that most matters pending before the Finance Commission will
involve the City directly, and others indirectly. As such, this financial interest (i.e. the city) will
be involved in virtually all matters, directly in most cases, considered and acted on by the
Finance Commission."


File No. I-10-069
Page No. 3
ANALYSIS
Steps 1. and 2: Is Commissioner Ezzeldine a "public official,, and will he be making,
participating in making, or influencing governmentaldecisions?
Your facts indicate that Steps I and} have been satisfied. You state that "As a member
of the Finance Commission, Commissioner Ezzeldine is a public official under the Act; and he
will be making or participating in making various governmental decisions in an advisory
capacity."
Please note that if the Finance Commission is solely advisory, then the commission's
members are not considered public officials for purposes of the Act and are not subject to the
Act's conflict-of-interest rules. (See Regulation 18701(a)(l).) However, because you state in
your facts that Commissioner Ezzeldine is a public official who is making, participating in
making or influencing a government decision, for pulposes of this analysls,'we assume that the
first two steps are satisfied.
step 3: what are commissioner Ezzerdine,s..economic interests?r,
A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section
87103 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her
immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated economic interests, including:
o An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or indirect
investment of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(a); Regulation lg703.l(a)); or in which he
or she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any posiiion of
management (Section 87103(d); Regulation 1 g703. l(b));
o An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect interest
of $ 2,000 or more (Section 87103(b); Regulation 19703.2);
o An economic interest in any source of income, including promised income, aggregating
$500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision lSeciion 87103(c); negulation
18703.3);
o An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts aggregate to $420 or
more within l2 months prior to the decision (Section 87103(e); R.grio-tion Lgj03.4):
o An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his or her
immediate family-this is the "personal financial effects" mle (Section 87103;
Regulation 18703.5).


File No. I-10-069
Page No.4
Business Entity: CommissionerEzzeldine has an economic interest in the summer camp
as a business entity, assuming he has a direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more. (Section
87103(a); Regulation 18703. t(a).)
Source of Income: Your main question focuses on whether the income Commissioner
Ezzeldine would receive from th-e cityltself-if he enters into an agreement to participate in the
parks and recreation programs offered by the city's CSD-would create a potential conflict with
his position as a member of the city Finance Commission. (Section 87103; Regulation 1g703.3.)
The Act's definition of income excludes "salary . . . received from a state, local, or
federal government agency," (Section 82030(bX2)), but that exception does not apply when, as
here, an individual receives income from a public agency as the result of an agreement or
contract and the individual does not, in relation to that income, serve in a capicity as a..public
official" as defined by the Act and Commission regulations. (Williams Advice Letter, wo. t-OS-
0s2.)
Therefore, any income Commissioner Ezzeldine receives through an agreement with the
city's CSD will not be subject to the "govemment salary exception" tolh. defLition of income.
Therefore, he would have a source of income economic intereit in the payments he receives from
CSD under an agreement or contract with that agency.
Personal Finances: Every public official has an economic interest in his or her personal
finances and those of his or her immediate family. (Section 87103; Regulation 1g703.i.) A
governmental decision will have an effect on this economic interest if the decision will result in
the official's or his or her immediate family's personal expenses, income, assets or liabilities,
other than the financial effect on your real property intereit or business, increasing or decreasing.
(lbid.) You have not provided any facts to suggest CommissionerEzzeldine's participation in
governmental decisions would affect his personal finances. We do not, therefoie, furtier analyze
his personal finances as a potential conflict of interest.
We restrict our analysis only to the question of whether he may participate in decisions as
a member of the city's Finance Commission affecting his source of income uni his business
interest in the summer camp, and his potential sourcJof income in the city's CSD, assuming he
enters into an agreement to participate in the city's parks and recreation piograms.
Step 4: Are Commissioner Lzzeldine's interests directly or indirectly involved in the
decision?
A source of income is directly involved in a decision before an official's agency when
that person, either directly or by an agent:
(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made by filing an
application, claim, appeal, or similar request; or


File No. I-10-069
Page No. 5
(2) ts a named party in, or is the subject of, the proceeding conceming the
decision before the official or the official's og"n"y. A perion is the sribject of a
proceeding if a decision involves the issuance, ,en"**I, approval, denial or
revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the
subject person. (Regularion 1 g704. l(a).)
lf Commissioner Ezzeldine's sllmmer camp or the city's CSD is a named party in,
initiates, or is the subject of the proceedings, they will be directly involved in the decisions.
otherwise, they will be indirectly involved. (Regularion tg7}4jtul.l
You state in your facts that if "the City itself is deemed to be a financial interest of
Commissionet Ezzeldine, then we recogni ze that most matters pending before the Finance
Commission will involve the city directly, and others indirectly. As Juch, this financial interest
(i.e. the City) will be involved in virtually all matters, directly in most cases, considered and
acted on by the Finance Commission." You do not provide specific decisions that would come
before the Finance Commission. You would need to analyzeiachdecision on a case-by-case
basis to determine whether CommissionerEzzeldine's economic interests are directly or
indirectly involved.
Step 5: What is the applicable materiality standard?
Generally' any reasonably foreseeable financial effect on a person who is a source of
income to a public official, and who is directly involved in a decision before the official,s
agency, is deemed material. This standard for determining materiality is known as the ,.one
penny" rule. If the entity is affected by even one penny, the financiaieffect is deemed material.
(Regulation 1 8705.3(a).)
The materiality standards for indirectly involved sources of income that are nonprofit
entities, including government agencies, Te set forth in Regulation 1g705.3(b)(2) (copy
enclosed), and depend on the gross annual receipts of the "itity.
Regulation 18705.3(b)(2XB) govems the applicable materiality standard for govemment
agencies whose gross annual receipts are more tha; $100,000,000 but less than g400]000,000.
The materiality threshold will be met if:
"(i) The decision will result in an increase or decrease of the entity's gross annual
receipts for a fiscal year in the amount of $400,000 or more; or
(ii) The decision will cause the entity to incur or avoid additional expenses or to
reduce or eliminate existing expenses for a fiscal year in the amount of $100,000
or more; or


File No. I-10-069
Page No. 6
(iii) The decision will result in an increase or decrease in the value of the entity's
assets or liabilities in the amount of $400,000 or more.,,
Step 6: Is the financial effect reasonably foreseeable?
A material financial effect on an economic interest is "reasonably foreseeable" if
it is substantially likely that one or more of the materiality standards wili be met as a
result of the governmental decision. (Regulation 18706(a).) An effect need not be certain
to be considered "reasonably foreseeable," but it must be more than a mere possibility.
(In re Thorner,l FPPC Ops. 198 (1975).) On the other hand, if an effect istnly a mere
possibility, it is not reasonably foreseeable.
You must apply this standard to each applicable decision and determine if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the materiality standard will be met.
Step 7: Public Generally Exception
The "public generally" exception allows an otherwise disqualified official to
participate in a decision despite a conflict of interest if the effect of the decision on the
official's interest is not distinguishable from the effect on the public generally. (Section
87103.)
Regulation 18707 states that the exception will apply if the decision will affect a
significant segment of the public generally in substantially ihe same rnanner as it would
affect the public official's interests.
Regulation 18707.1(b)(lXD) states that decisions that affect a federal, state, or
local govemment in which the public official has an economic interest (such as the city,s
CSD), will fall under the public generally exception if the decision will affect all
members of the public under the jurisdiction of that governmental entity.
The comment at the end of Regulation 18707.1 provides: "The term 'affect all
members of the public' is intended to cover decisions affecting the public in general but
to exclude decisions which uniquely benefit a public official."
Based upon these provisions, you will have to analyze each decision before the
Finance Commission to determine whether it will "uniqueiy affect" Commissioner
Ezzeldine- For instance, with regard to decisions involving funding for activities and
programs' or cost savings measures, if Commissioner Ezzeldine or his business is not
uniquely affected (e.g., the decision does not have a unique or specific effect on his
agreement or contract with, or income from CSD or his summer camp), then the public
generally exception will apply and he may participate in the decision.


File No. I-10-069
Page No. 7
Step 8: Legal Required Exception
The "legally required participation" rule applies when the official's participation
in a governmental decision is legally required. (Siition 87101: Regularion iSzog.) you
have not presented any facts that pertain to this exception. We therefore do not address it.
Your facts indicate that while the decision will affect the City of Laguna Niguel, the decision
will benefit all the citizens in the city by increasing the city'; r"r"n,i" from iroperty taxes.
Furthermore, the decision will not uniquely affect -Commiisione r Ezzeldine. hi. ,po,rr", or his
spouse's business. T!m, the public generally exception would apply and
CommissionerEzzeldine may participate in the decision.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division 3
ERjgl
Enclosure

File No. I-10-069 Page No. 2 CONCLUSION Based on the facts you provided, Commissioner Ezzeldine will not have a disqualifying conflict of interest if the "public generally" exception applies as discussed below. FACTS You are the city attomey for the City of Irvine (the "city"). You are requesting informal advice on behalf of Omar Ezzeldine, an appointed member of the Finance Commission of the city. The City of Irvine has annual revenues in excess of $100,000,000 but less than $400,000,000. You wish to know whether Commissioner Ezzeldine's operation of the summer camp as part of the city's park and recreation programs (through the city's Commgnity Services Department) would create potential conflicts of interest for him in his duties as a member of the Finance Commission. Mt.Ezzeldine serves as an appointed member of the City of Irvine Finance Commission. The Finance Commission, which was established by city council resolution in 1986, and is an advisory body that makes recommendations to the City Council regarding: (l) the City's proposed budget; (2) the appropriation of funds; (3) funding for activities and programs; (4) potential revenue opportunities; (5) cost saving measures; (6) proposed support oior opposition to state or federal legislation; and (7) other matters as requested by the city council.
